citations.narrative:
The facility has one enclosure housing five grey squirrels and one fox squirrel that is less than three feet from the
perimeter fence. The enclosure is approximately 6 inches away from the perimeter fencing. Failure to provide an
appropriate perimeter fence may result in animal escape or allow persons or other animals to interact with the regulated
animals. All housing facilities for regulated animals must be enclosed by a perimeter fence that is of sufficient height to
keep animals and unauthorized person out. Fences less than 8 feet high for potentially dangerous animals, such as but
not limited to, large felines (e.g., lions, tigers, leopards, cougars, etc.), bears, wolves, rhinoceros, and elephants, or less
than 6 feet high for the other animals must be approved in writing by the administrator. It must be of sufficient distance
from the outside of the primary enclosure to prevent physical contact between animals inside the enclosure and animals
or person outside the perimeter fence. Such fences less than 3 feet in distance from the primary enclosure must be
approved in writing by the Administrator. Correct by ensuring that all species requiring a perimeter fence are enclosed
with an adequate perimeter fence or obtain an approved variance.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
Additional Inspectors:
Jonathan Tomkovitch, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
3.127(d)  Repeat
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
The facility has one enclosure housing five grey squirrels and one fox squirrel that is less than three feet from the 
perimeter fence. The enclosure is approximately 6 inches away from the perimeter fencing. Failure to provide an 
appropriate perimeter fence may result in animal escape or allow persons or other animals to interact with the regulated 
animals. All housing facilities for regulated animals must be enclosed by a perimeter fence that is of sufficient height to 
keep animals and unauthorized person out. Fences less than 8 feet high for potentially dangerous animals, such as but 
not limited to, large felines (e.g., lions, tigers, leopards, cougars, etc.), bears, wolves, rhinoceros, and elephants, or less 
than 6 feet high for the other animals must be approved in writing by the administrator. It must be of sufficient distance 
from the outside of the primary enclosure to prevent physical contact between animals inside the enclosure and animals 
or person outside the perimeter fence. Such fences less than 3 feet in distance from the primary enclosure must be 
approved in writing by the Administrator. Correct by ensuring that all species requiring a perimeter fence are enclosed

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
Jonathan Tomkovitch, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n
